user_prompt:
I am trying to upload files in our Google Workspace, but one project area is acting read-only and I also saw a storage warning.
Can you help me figure out what this means and what I should do next?

content.content:

If required information, user authorization, or safe parameters are missing, call get_user_help.

I am trying to upload files in our Google Workspace, but one project area is acting read-only and I also saw a storage warning.
Can you help me figure out what this means and what I should do next?

args.reason: Determine whether your Workspace role and permissions allow uploading files or changing the affected project area.
Check Workspace storage, subscription, Drive restrictions, and policy conditions that could make an area read-only.
Check whether there is an active upload or project-area restriction, its blocking reason, and the safe next step.

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Google Workspace storage FAQ for admins

  - url: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/storage/google-workspace-storage-faq-for-admins

  - source: Google Workspace Help

  - domain: knowledge.workspace.google.com


  ## Content


  # Google Workspace storage FAQ for admins


  Google Workspace storage is shared across Google Drive, Gmail, and Google Photos.
  Most Workspace editions use pooled storage, where licenses contribute to a combined
  organizational storage pool. The amount available depends on the Workspace edition,
  the number and type of licenses, and any additional storage attached to the organization.


  Administrators can review organization-wide storage use in the Admin console. The
  storage page shows the total limit and how use is distributed across users, shared
  drives, and Workspace services. Access to detailed storage information requires
  the appropriate administrator privilege.


  ## What happens when a storage limit is exceeded


  A user who exceeds a storage limit assigned by an administrator or by a license
  can experience immediate service restrictions.


  Organization-wide pooled storage enforcement follows a different timeline. When
  an organization first exceeds its pooled storage limit, Google Photos additions
  are affected first. Drive and collaborative-content restrictions apply when the
  organization has exceeded its limit by 25 percent or has remained over the limit
  for 14 days, whichever happens first.


  At that enforcement point, users cannot add new files or images to Google Drive
  or create new files in Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drawings, or Forms. Affected files
  can still be viewed and downloaded, while some editing, copying, form submission,
  and Meet recording functions are restricted until storage use is reduced.


  An organization that is only slightly over its pooled limit for a short period has
  not necessarily reached the Drive enforcement condition described above. Administrators
  should review the actual storage amount, the date the organization first exceeded
  the limit, and any separate user or shared-drive limits before attributing an upload
  failure to pooled-storage enforcement.


  Drive also has separate upload and item limits. Those limits are distinct from organization-wide
  pooled storage and should be evaluated independently when the error or status indicates
  a different restriction.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Google Workspace storage FAQ for admins
url: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/storage/google-workspace-storage-faq-for-admins

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Turn file creation & upload on or off for users

  - url: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/drive/turn-file-creation-and-upload-on-or-off-for-users

  - source: Google Workspace Help

  - domain: knowledge.workspace.google.com


  ## Content


  # Turn file creation & upload on or off for users


  Google Workspace administrators can control whether users in an organization, organizational
  unit, or configuration group are allowed to create and upload files in Drive. File
  creation and upload are normally enabled, but an administrator can turn the setting
  off for selected users or teams.


  When file creation and upload is turned off, users cannot create or copy Docs, Sheets,
  Slides, Forms, Drawings, Sites, Vids, or email layouts. They also cannot upload
  non-Google files such as PDFs, images, or Microsoft Office documents. Existing files
  can still be moved, edited, shared, viewed, or downloaded, depending on the user''s
  normal Drive permissions.


  The setting is managed in the Google Admin console under Drive and Docs, Features
  and Applications. An administrator can apply the setting to the whole organization
  or to a specific organizational unit. A group or organizational-unit override may
  therefore affect one project area or team while other areas continue to allow uploads.


  Changing this setting requires the Service Settings administrator privilege. A non-admin
  user who encounters this condition can report the affected area and error details,
  but cannot change the workspace-level setting directly.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Turn file creation & upload on or off for users
url: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/drive/turn-file-creation-and-upload-on-or-off-for-users

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Review storage use across your organization | Set up & manage services
  | Google Workspace Help

  - url: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/storage/review-storage-use-across-your-organization

  - source: Google Workspace Help

  - domain: knowledge.workspace.google.com


  ## Content


  # Review storage use across your organization


  Administrators can use storage management tools in the Google Admin console to review
  storage use, see the organization''s total storage limit, and set storage limits
  for users and shared drives. The storage view shows how storage is used across individuals,
  shared drives, and Google Workspace services.


  Most Google Workspace editions have pooled storage. With pooled storage, storage
  is shared across users in the organization. The total storage limit is the maximum
  amount that all users combined can use. It depends on the storage included with
  each license, how many licenses the organization has, and any Google Workspace Additional
  Storage that has been purchased.


  ## See how storage is used in your organization


  1. In the Google Admin console, go to Storage.

  2. Review how much Workspace storage the organization is using and the total storage
  limit.

  3. Point to the services to see how much storage Google Drive, Google Photos, and
  Gmail each use.


  Using the storage page requires the Storage administrator privilege. If an organization
  cleared space or bought storage, it can take up to 24 hours for changes in storage
  to be reflected on the Admin console storage page. A storage status bar for the
  entire organization can reflect some changes within 4 hours when an admin checks
  Drive quota with an admin account.


  ## Identify high-storage users


  The Users using the most storage section gives a summary of high-storage users.
  Administrators can open a list of all users ranked by storage use and can view details
  of storage use for a specific user. For privacy, an administrator cannot see a user''s
  files through the Admin console; the console shows only a summary of storage used
  by users'' documents and files.


  ## Identify high-storage shared drives


  The Shared drives using the most storage section gives a summary of high-storage
  shared drives. Administrators can open a list of shared drives ranked by storage
  use. Administrators can also manage how much pooled storage each shared drive can
  use.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Review storage use across your organization | Set up & manage services | Google
  Workspace Help
url: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/storage/review-storage-use-across-your-organization

This looks like **two separate restrictions**:

1. **The project area is explicitly read-only for new content.**  
   The current status reports `FILE_CREATION_UPLOAD_DISABLED`: creating or uploading files is disabled for that project area while its Drive settings are under review. Existing files should remain viewable, and possibly editable or downloadable depending on permissions.

2. **Your Workspace is over its pooled storage limit.**  
   The organization is using approximately **104%** of its pooled storage, and exceeded the limit three days ago. Google’s documented pooled-storage enforcement generally affects Drive creation/upload when the organization is **25% over quota or has remained over quota for 14 days**, whichever comes first. Therefore, the storage warning is important, but it may not yet be the direct cause of this project area’s read-only behavior. A separate organizational-unit or project-area setting is the more immediate explanation.

### What you should do next

- **Contact your Workspace administrator** and provide:
  - The affected project/shared-drive area
  - The exact error code: `FILE_CREATION_UPLOAD_DISABLED`
  - When the issue began
  - Whether uploads work elsewhere
  - The storage warning and current usage of 104%

- Ask the administrator to check:
  1. **Admin console → Drive and Docs → Features and Applications**  
     Confirm that file creation and upload are enabled for the affected organizational unit or configuration group.
  2. Whether the project area is under a temporary **Drive settings review** or has a project/shared-drive restriction.
  3. **Admin console → Storage**  
     Identify the largest users, shared drives, Gmail, Drive, and Photos usage.
  4. Whether they need to remove unnecessary data, adjust shared-drive limits, add storage, or otherwise reduce usage.

- **Do not delete or move project files on your own** unless your organization’s retention and ownership rules allow it. Ask the administrator or project owner to manage cleanup.

- In the meantime, use an approved alternative location only if your administrator confirms it is permitted. Existing files in the affected area can still be viewed; downloading them may be possible, but avoid creating duplicate copies that worsen storage usage.

You are a project manager without Admin console, billing, security, or app-management privileges, so you cannot fix either the organizational storage condition or the upload setting directly. Google’s guidance confirms that administrators control file-creation/upload settings by organizational unit and review pooled storage from the Admin console.

final_answer:
This looks like **two separate restrictions**:

1. **The project area is explicitly read-only for new content.**  
   The current status reports `FILE_CREATION_UPLOAD_DISABLED`: creating or uploading files is disabled for that project area while its Drive settings are under review. Existing files should remain viewable, and possibly editable or downloadable depending on permissions.

2. **Your Workspace is over its pooled storage limit.**  
   The organization is using approximately **104%** of its pooled storage, and exceeded the limit three days ago. Google’s documented pooled-storage enforcement generally affects Drive creation/upload when the organization is **25% over quota or has remained over quota for 14 days**, whichever comes first. Therefore, the storage warning is important, but it may not yet be the direct cause of this project area’s read-only behavior. A separate organizational-unit or project-area setting is the more immediate explanation.

### What you should do next

- **Contact your Workspace administrator** and provide:
  - The affected project/shared-drive area
  - The exact error code: `FILE_CREATION_UPLOAD_DISABLED`
  - When the issue began
  - Whether uploads work elsewhere
  - The storage warning and current usage of 104%

- Ask the administrator to check:
  1. **Admin console → Drive and Docs → Features and Applications**  
     Confirm that file creation and upload are enabled for the affected organizational unit or configuration group.
  2. Whether the project area is under a temporary **Drive settings review** or has a project/shared-drive restriction.
  3. **Admin console → Storage**  
     Identify the largest users, shared drives, Gmail, Drive, and Photos usage.
  4. Whether they need to remove unnecessary data, adjust shared-drive limits, add storage, or otherwise reduce usage.

- **Do not delete or move project files on your own** unless your organization’s retention and ownership rules allow it. Ask the administrator or project owner to manage cleanup.

- In the meantime, use an approved alternative location only if your administrator confirms it is permitted. Existing files in the affected area can still be viewed; downloading them may be possible, but avoid creating duplicate copies that worsen storage usage.

You are a project manager without Admin console, billing, security, or app-management privileges, so you cannot fix either the organizational storage condition or the upload setting directly. Google’s guidance confirms that administrators control file-creation/upload settings by organizational unit and review pooled storage from the Admin console.
